rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: KRYSTAL MICHELLE STEVENS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 2029.600
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, December 6, 2024, line 1, PETITIONERS KRYSTAL STEVENS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF ELIJAH ALEXANDER AULD, DECEASED, JOHN AULD, JOSEPH STEVENS' Notice Of Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 2029.600 Plaintiff-Intervenors' Petition Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 2029.600 is GRANTED. Movant Technologies, Inc. ("Movant") is ordered to comply with the subpoena and produce all responsive documents. The record supports issuance of the order. Defendant PalletOne of Texas, L.P.'s objections are overruled. The objections are untimely. Moreover, the application is the operative pleading in this proceeding and a proof of service of summons is on file. This court has jurisdiction over Plaintiff-Intervenors' California subpoena served upon Movant. No other court first exercised proper jurisdiction over this subpoena. The period covered by Plaintiff-Intervenors' request is reasonable. The requests are not unduly burdensome. PalletOne's response to a similar discovery request from other Plaintiffs does not render this request-addressed to a different party-improper, unreasonable or undue. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. = (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAY EPPS VS. SHEIN US SERVICES, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, December 6, 2024, line 9, PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment The motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is denied without prejudice. In light of the proposed amendents to the settlement's terms, Plaintiff may file a new motion that allows the court to make its required findings per Health & Safety Code 25249.7(f)(4)-(5). rulings.Case Title: JOSE MARIA DEL CID VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, December 6, 2024, line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendant City and County of San Franciso's demurrer to Plaintiff Jose Maria Del Cid's "Initial Complaint" is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff's "Initial Complaint" is not a proper pleading for the relief that plaintiff seeks. Plaintiff has leave to file a petition for relief (not a complaint) in compliance with Government Code section 946.6 supported by competent evidence (e.g., not inadmissible hearsay). (Dept. of Water & Power v. Superior Court (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1288, 1291 ["Relief from failure to timely present a government tort claim is available only if the applicant establishes by a preponderance of the evidence the failure was 'through mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect.'"].) Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. = (302/JMQ)
